slides.title: 03-YANG Groupings for QUIC clients and QUIC servers
06-CBOR Encoding for HTTPS-based YANG Notifications Transport
08-Error List and Error Identities Registries for YANG-driven protocols
09-Requirements for Network State Exchange in Agent-Assisted Netops

polls.text: Do you think the Error Registry document should be adopted?

chat.author: Bharadwaja Chittapragada
chat.text:
Hi Kent and Per, 
Just wanted to mention that -  CBOR Encoding for HTTPS-based YANG Notifications Transport (10 min)

will be presenting remote - incase that was missed

chat.author: Pierre Francois
chat.text: @rob ok no problem, we'll change asap.

chat.author: Mahesh Jethanandani
chat.text: An early TSVART review would be great.

chat.author: Per Andersson
chat.text: It is gold to have this comment of blissful ignorance on record @rob :)

chat.author: Mahesh Jethanandani
chat.text: Just to confirm that for the list pagination drafts, I will run another WGLC once the authors have made the changes and Rob has reviewed the document.

chat.author: Thomas Graf
chat.text: @Meher, many thanks to the CBOR named identifier contribution to libyang. This is very well received.

chat.author: Per Andersson
chat.text: @meetecho can you move the camera to the speaker please

chat.author: Bharadwaja Chittapragada
chat.text:
Thanks @Mahesh @Kent and @Alex Huang Feng and @Thomas for the upstream updates, reviews,  suggestions and guidance
We'll also take at a look at the .sid file and its allocation

chat.author: Alex Huang Feng
chat.text: I agree with Thomas, I would welcome to use YPath widely in the YANG environment. So I welcome having this in a different document.

chat.author: Holger Keller
chat.text: @rob if you create a message for flapping (instead of sending 10000 times the new state), there is also a message needed, that flapping is not occuring. at least traditional event-systems need these messages to be able  correlate against the flap start

chat.author: Reshad Rahman
chat.text: @Rob apologies for missing your presentation, my AI agent sent me to the DAWN meeting

chat.author: Mahesh Jethanandani
chat.text: I also agree that the error registries document is useful and should be adopted.

chat.author: Mahesh Jethanandani
chat.text: @Per, I am not sure I understood the comment on YANG module/IANA registry. Are you saying it will be a IANA YANG module with an IANA registry backing it to record all errors?

chat.author: Per Andersson
chat.text: @Mahesh: Yes, both. We create IANA registries to be able to generate IANA maintained YANG modules. In my view it seems like a simple mode of operation for IANA to maintain everything.

chat.author: Per Andersson
chat.text: Thank you for this session, see you next time!
